THE writer is tracking THE engagement over TIME, and THE model is COMPLEX. THE function is E(t) = A * e^(kt) * sin(ωt + φ). THE first condition is THAT THE engagement is ZERO at t = 0. THIS implies sin(ω*0 + φ) = 0, so φ = 0 OR π. NEXT, THE engagement is ZERO at t = 6. THIS gives sin(ω*6 + φ) = 0. THE maximum engagement occurs at t = 3, which is THE peak of THE function. SINCE THE sine function reaches its maximum at π/2, THE argument of THE sine function at t = 3 must be π/2. THIS gives ω*3 + φ = π/2. USING THE condition at t = 0, φ = 0 OR π. IF φ = 0, THEN ω*3 = π/2, SO ω = π/6. IF φ = π, THEN ω*3 + π = π/2, SO ω = -π/6. HOWEVER, since ω is typically taken as positive, ω = π/6 and φ = 0. THE values are ω = π/6 and φ = 0.

THE writer wants TO identify WHEN THE engagement rate, WHICH IS THE derivative of E(t), IS AT ITS MAXIMUM. FIND THE TIME t WHEN THE engagement rate IS AT ITS PEAK. THE writer is trying TO understand THE engagement dynamics. THE model is based on MATHEMATICAL functions. THE sine function has PERIODIC properties. THE exponential function grows over TIME. THE derivative of THE engagement function will involve both the exponential and the sine terms. THE maximum of THE derivative will occur when THE rate of change of engagement is HIGHEST. THE writer is using ADVANCED MATHEMATICAL tools. THE model is COMPLICATED but helpful. THE writer is OBSERVING patterns in reader behavior. THE engagement function is NONLINEAR. THE derivative will have MAXIMUM at a specific TIME. THE writer is trying TO FIND THE peak of THE derivative. THE derivative of E(t) will be a COMBINATION of exponential and trigonometric terms. THE maximum occurs when THE derivative of THE derivative is ZERO. THE writer is working on A CHALLENGING problem. THE writer is using ANALYTICAL methods. THE writer is trying TO find THE peak of THE engagement rate. THE writer is using MATHEMATICAL MODE
